scan_output: Vulnerability chronyd_specify_remote_server not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
The `/etc/chrony.conf` file already contains a remote time source (`pool 2.rhel.pool.ntp.org iburst`). No modifications were required, and the Chrony daemon was restarted to ensure the existing configuration is active.

**Result:** The OpenSCAP finding **chronyd_specify_remote_server** is now remediated.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
Check /etc/chrony.conf for an active server or pool line. If one exists (uncommented), the finding is already satisfied. If none are present, add a remote server entry. Use a generic pool such as “0.pool.ntp.org iburst” (or a placeholder servertime.example.com”) and ensure it is uncommented. If any server lines are commented, uncomment them; otherwise append the new line at the end of the file. This satisfies the requirement that chrony has a remote server configured.

Read the chrony config, look for an active server or pool line. If none, search for commented server lines; if found, uncomment them with sed. If no server lines exist at all, append “server 0.pool.ntp.org iburst”. After modifying the file, restart chronyd. Use read_file to inspect, run_cmd for sed edits, and service restart.
